v.CNV: 有一个安息日，耶稣从麦田经过，他的门徒摘了麦穗，用手搓着吃。
v.ESV: On a Sabbath, while he was going through the grainfields, his disciples plucked and ate some heads of grain, rubbing them in their hands.
v.GAE: 안식일에 예수께서 밀밭 사이로 지나가실새 제자들이 이삭을 잘라 손으로 비비어 먹으니
v.KJV: And it came to pass on the second sabbath after the first, that he went through the corn fields; and his disciples plucked the ears of corn, and did eat, rubbing them in their hands.
v.KRV: 안식일에 예수께서 밀밭 사이로 지나가실쌔 제자들이 이삭을 잘라 손으로 비비어 먹으니
v.NASB: Now it happened that He was passing through some grainfields on a Sabbath; and His disciples were picking the heads of grain, rubbing them in their hands, and eating the grain.
v.NIV: One Sabbath Jesus was going through the grainfields, and his disciples began to pick some heads of grain, rub them in their hands and eat the kernels.
v.NRSV: One sabbath while Jesus was going through the grainfields, his disciples plucked some heads of grain, rubbed them in their hands, and ate them.
v.SAENEW: 한 안식일에 예수께서 밀밭 사이로 지나가시게 되었다. 그런데 그의 제자들이 밀 이삭을 잘라, 손으로 비벼서 먹었다.
v.WLB: 안식일에 예수께서 밀밭을 지나가시는데 제자들이 밀 이삭을 잘라 손으로 비벼서 먹었습니다.

v.CNV: 有几个法利赛人说：“你们为什么作安息日不可作的事呢？”
v.ESV: But some of the Pharisees said, “Why are you doing what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath?”
v.GAE: 어떤 바리새인들이 말하되 어찌하여 안식일에 하지 못할 일을 하느냐
v.KJV: And certain of the Pharisees said unto them, Why do ye that which is not lawful to do on the sabbath days?
v.KRV: 어떤 바리새인들이 말하되 어찌하여 안식일에 하지 못할 일을 하느뇨
v.NASB: But some of the Pharisees said, 'Why do you do what is not lawful on the Sabbath?'
v.NIV: Some of the Pharisees asked, “Why are you doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?”
v.NRSV: But some of the Pharisees said, “Why are you doing what is not lawful on the sabbath?”
v.SAENEW: 그러자 몇몇 바리새파 사람이 말하였다. "어찌하여 당신들은 안식일에 해서는 안 되는 일을 합니까?"
v.WLB: 그러자 몇몇 바리새파 사람들이 말했습니다. “당신들은 왜 안식일에 해서는 안 될 일을 하는 것이오?”

v.CNV: 耶稣回答：“大卫和跟他在一起的人，在饥饿的时候所作的，你们没有念过吗？
v.ESV: And Jesus answered them, “Have you not read what David did when he was hungry, he and those who were with him:
v.GAE: 예수께서 대답하여 이르시되 다윗이 자기 및 자기와 함께 한 자들이 시장할 때에 한 일을 읽지 못하였느냐
v.KJV: And Jesus answering them said, Have ye not read so much as this, what David did, when himself was an hungred, and they which were with him;
v.KRV: 예수께서 대답하여 가라사대 다윗이 자기와 및 함께한 자들이 시장할 때에 한 일을 읽지 못하였느냐
v.NASB: And Jesus answering them said, 'Have you not even read what David did when he was hungry, he and those who were with him,
v.NIV: Jesus answered them, “Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?
v.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 대답하셨다. "다윗과 그 일행이 주렸을 때에, 다윗이 한 일을 너희는 읽어보지 못하였느냐?
v.WLB: 예수께서 그들에게 대답하셨습니다. “다윗과 그 일행이 굶주렸을 때 다윗이 한 일을 읽어 보지 못했느냐?

v.CNV: 他不是进了　神的殿，吃了陈设饼，也给跟他在一起的人吃吗？这饼除了祭司以外，别的人是不可以吃的。”
v.ESV: how he entered the house of God and took and ate the bread of the Presence, which is not lawful for any but the priests to eat, and also gave it to those with him?”
v.GAE: 그가 하나님의 전에 들어가서 다만 제사장 외에는 먹어서는 안 되는 진설병을 먹고 함께 한 자들에게도 주지 아니하였느냐
v.KJV: How he went into the house of God, and did take and eat the shewbread, and gave also to them that were with him; which it is not lawful to eat but for the priests alone?
v.KRV: 그가 하나님의 전에 들어가서 다만 제사장 외에는 먹지 못하는 진설병을 집어 먹고 함께한 자들에게도 주지 아니하였느냐
v.NASB: how he entered the house of God, and took and ate the consecrated bread which is not lawful for any to eat except the priests alone, and gave it to his companions?'
v.NIV: He entered the house of God, and taking the consecrated bread, he ate what is lawful only for priests to eat. And he also gave some to his companions.”
v.NRSV: He entered the house of God and took and ate the bread of the Presence, which it is not lawful for any but the priests to eat, and gave some to his companions?”
v.SAENEW: 다윗이 하나님의 집에 들어가서, 제사장들 밖에는 먹어서는 안 되는 제단 빵을 집어서 먹고, 자기 일행에게도 주지 않았느냐?"
v.WLB: 다윗이 하나님의 집에 들어가 제사장만 먹게 돼 있는 진설병을 자신이 먹고 또 자기 일행에게도 나눠 주지 않았느냐?”

v.CNV: 他又对他们说：“人子是安息日的主。”
v.ESV: And he said to them, “The Son of Man is lord of the Sabbath.”
v.GAE: 또 이르시되 인자는 안식일의 주인이니라 하시더라
v.KJV: And he said unto them, That the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.
v.KRV: 또 가라사대 인자는 안식일의 주인이니라 하시더라
v.NASB: And He was saying to them, 'The Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.'
v.NIV: Then Jesus said to them, “The Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.”
v.NRSV: Then he said to them, “The Son of Man is lord of the sabbath.”
v.SAENEW: 그리고 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "인자는 안식일의 주인이다."
v.WLB: 그러고 나서 예수께서 바리새파 사람들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “인자는 안식일의 주인이다.”

v.CNV: 另一个安息日，耶稣进入会堂教导人，在那里有一个人，右手枯干，
v.ESV: On another Sabbath, he entered the synagogue and was teaching, and a man was there whose right hand was withered.
v.GAE: 또 다른 안식일에 예수께서 회당에 들어가사 가르치실새 거기 오른손 마른 사람이 있는지라
v.KJV: And it came to pass also on another sabbath, that he entered into the synagogue and taught: and there was a man whose right hand was withered.
v.KRV: 또 다른 안식일에 예수께서 회당에 들어가사 가르치실쌔 거기 오른손 마른 사람이 있는지라
v.NASB: On another Sabbath He entered the synagogue and was teaching; and there was a man there whose right hand was withered.
v.NIV: On another Sabbath he went into the synagogue and was teaching, and a man was there whose right hand was shriveled.
v.NRSV: On another sabbath he entered the synagogue and taught, and there was a man there whose right hand was withered.
v.SAENEW: 또 다른 안식일에 예수께서 회당에 들어가서 가르치시는데, 거기에는 오른손이 오그라든 사람이 있었다.
v.WLB: 또 다른 안식일에 예수께서 회당에 들어가 가르치셨는데 거기에 오른손이 오그라든 사람이 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 经学家和法利赛人要看他会不会在安息日治病，好找把柄控告他。
v.ESV: And the scribes and the Pharisees watched him, to see whether he would heal on the Sabbath, so that they might find a reason to accuse him.
v.GAE: 서기관과 바리새인들이 예수를 고발할 증거를 찾으려 하여 안식일에 병을 고치시는가 엿보니
v.KJV: And the scribes and Pharisees watched him, whether he would heal on the sabbath day; that they might find an accusation against him.
v.KRV: 서기관과 바리새인들이 예수를 송사할 빙거를 찾으려 하여 안식일에 병 고치시는가 엿보니
v.NASB: The scribes and the Pharisees were watching Him closely to see if He healed on the Sabbath, so that they might find reason to accuse Him.
v.NIV: The Pharisees and the teachers of the law were looking for a reason to accuse Jesus, so they watched him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
v.NRSV: The scribes and the Pharisees watched him to see whether he would cure on the sabbath, so that they might find an accusation against him.
v.SAENEW: 율법학자들과 바리새파 사람들은 예수를 고발할 구실을 찾으려고, 예수가 안식일에 병을 고치시는지 엿보고 있었다.
v.WLB: 바리새파 사람들과 율법학자들은 예수를 고소할 구실을 찾으려고 안식일에 예수께서 병을 고치시는지 안 고치시는지 엿보고 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣知道他们的意念，就对那一只手枯干了的人说：“起来，站在当中！”那人就起来站着。
v.ESV: But he knew their thoughts, and he said to the man with the withered hand, “Come and stand here.” And he rose and stood there.
v.GAE: 예수께서 그들의 생각을 아시고 손 마른 사람에게 이르시되 일어나 한가운데 서라 하시니 그가 일어나 서거늘
v.KJV: But he knew their thoughts, and said to the man which had the withered hand, Rise up, and stand forth in the midst. And he arose and stood forth.
v.KRV: 예수께서 저희 생각을 아시고 손 마른 사람에게 이르시되 일어나 한가운데 서라 하시니 저가 일어나 서거늘
v.NASB: But He knew what they were thinking, and He said to the man with the withered hand, 'Get up and come forward!' And he got up and came forward.
v.NIV: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said to the man with the shriveled hand, “Get up and stand in front of everyone.” So he got up and stood there.
v.NRSV: Even though he knew what they were thinking, he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Come and stand here.” He got up and stood there.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들의 생각을 아시고, 손이 오그라든 사람에게 말씀하셨다. "일어나서, 가운데 서라." 그래서 그는 일어나서 섰다.
v.WLB: 그러나 예수께서는 그들의 속마음을 꿰뚫어 보시고 손이 오그라든 사람에게 말씀하셨습니다. “일어나 앞으로 나오너라!” 그러자 그가 일어나 앞으로 나왔습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣对他们说：“我问你们：在安息日哪一样是可以作的呢：作好事还是坏事？救命还是害命？”
v.ESV: And Jesus said to them, “I ask you, is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do harm, to save life or to destroy it?”
v.GAE: 예수께서 그들에게 이르시되 내가 너희에게 묻노니 안식일에 선을 행하는 것과 악을 행하는 것, 생명을 구하는 것과 죽이는 것, 어느 것이 옳으냐 하시며
v.KJV: Then said Jesus unto them, I will ask you one thing; Is it lawful on the sabbath days to do good, or to do evil? to save life, or to destroy it?
v.KRV: 예수께서 저희에게 이르시되 내가 너희에게 묻노니 안식일에 선을 행하는 것과 악을 행하는 것 생명을 구하는 것과 멸하는 것 어느 것이 옳으냐 하시며
v.NASB: And Jesus said to them, 'I ask you, is it lawful to do good or to do harm on the Sabbath, to save a life or to destroy it?'
v.NIV: Then Jesus said to them, “I ask you, which is lawful on the Sabbath: to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it?”
v.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “I ask you, is it lawful to do good or to do harm on the sabbath, to save life or to destroy it?”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "너희에게 물어 보겠다. 안식일에 착한 일을 하는 것이 옳으냐? 악한 일을 하는 것이 옳으냐? 목숨을 건지는 것이 옳으냐? 죽이는 것이 옳으냐?"
v.WLB: 그리고 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “내가 너희에게 묻겠다. 안식일에 선한 일을 하는 것과 악한 일을 하는 것 중 어느 것이 옳으냐? 사람을 살리는 것과 죽이는 것 중 어느 것이 옳으냐?”

v.CNV: 他环视周围所有的人，就对那人说：“伸出你的手来！”他把手一伸，手就复原了。
v.ESV: And after looking around at them all he said to him, “Stretch out your hand.” And he did so, and his hand was restored.
v.GAE: 무리를 둘러보시고 그 사람에게 이르시되 네 손을 내밀라 하시니 그가 그리하매 그 손이 회복된지라
v.KJV: And looking round about upon them all, he said unto the man, Stretch forth thy hand. And he did so: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
v.KRV: 무리를 둘러 보시고 그 사람에게 이르시되 네 손을 내밀라 하시니 저가 그리하매 그 손이 회복된지라
v.NASB: After looking around at them all, He said to him, 'Stretch out your hand!' And he did so; and his hand was restored.
v.NIV: He looked around at them all, and then said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did so, and his hand was completely restored.
v.NRSV: After looking around at all of them, he said to him, “Stretch out your hand.” He did so, and his hand was restored.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들을 모두 둘러보시고서, 그 사람에게 명하셨다. "네 손을 내밀어라." 그 사람이 그렇게 하니, 그의 손이 회복되었다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 그들 모두를 둘러보고는 그 사람에게 말씀하셨습니다. “네 손을 펴 보아라!” 그가 손을 펴자 그의 손이 회복됐습니다.

v.CNV: 他们却大怒，彼此商议怎样对付耶稣。
v.ESV: But they were filled with fury and discussed with one another what they might do to Jesus.
v.GAE: 그들은 노기가 가득하여 예수를 어떻게 할까 하고 서로 의논하니라
v.KJV: And they were filled with madness; and communed one with another what they might do to Jesus.
v.KRV: 저희는 분기가 가득하여 예수를 어떻게 처치할 것을 서로 의논하니라
v.NASB: But they themselves were filled with rage, and discussed together what they might do to Jesus.
v.NIV: But the Pharisees and the teachers of the law were furious and began to discuss with one another what they might do to Jesus.
v.NRSV: But they were filled with fury and discussed with one another what they might do to Jesus.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 화가 잔뜩 나서, 예수를 어떻게 할까 하고 서로 의논하였다.
v.WLB: 그러나 그들은 화가 나서 예수를 어떻게 해야 할지 서로 의논했습니다.

v.CNV: 在那些日子，有一次耶稣出去到山上祷告，整夜祷告　神。
v.ESV: In these days he went out to the mountain to pray, and all night he continued in prayer to God.
v.GAE: 이 때에 예수께서 기도하시러 산으로 가사 밤이 새도록 하나님께 기도하시고
v.KJV: And it came to pass in those days, that he went out into a mountain to pray, and continued all night in prayer to God.
v.KRV: 이 때에 예수께서 기도하시러 산으로 가사 밤이 맟도록 하나님께 기도하시고
v.NASB: It was at this time that He went off to the mountain to pray, and He spent the whole night in prayer to God.
v.NIV: One of those days Jesus went out to a mountainside to pray, and spent the night praying to God.
v.NRSV: Now during those days he went out to the mountain to pray; and he spent the night in prayer to God.
v.SAENEW: 그 무렵에 예수께서 기도하려고 산으로 떠나가서, 밤을 새우면서 하나님께 기도하셨다.
v.WLB: 그 무렵 예수께서 기도하시기 위해 산으로 올라가 밤을 새워 하나님께 기도하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 天亮以后，他把门徒叫来，从他们中间挑选了十二个人，称他们为使徒，
v.ESV: And when day came, he called his disciples and chose from them twelve, whom he named apostles:
v.GAE: 밝으매 그 제자들을 부르사 그 중에서 열둘을 택하여 사도라 칭하셨으니
v.KJV: And when it was day, he called unto him his disciples: and of them he chose twelve, whom also he named apostles;
v.KRV: 밝으매 그 제자들을 부르사 그 중에서 열 둘을 택하여 사도라 칭하셨으니
v.NASB: And when day came, He called His disciples to Him and chose twelve of them, whom He also named as apostles:
v.NIV: When morning came, he called his disciples to him and chose twelve of them, whom he also designated apostles:
v.NRSV: And when day came, he called his disciples and chose twelve of them, whom he also named apostles:
v.SAENEW: 날이 밝을 때에, 예수께서 자기의 제자들을 부르시고, 그 가운데서 열둘을 뽑으셨다. 그는 그들을 사도라고도 부르셨는데,
v.WLB: 날이 밝자 예수께서는 제자들을 불러 그중 12명을 뽑아 사도로 부르셨습니다.

v.CNV: 就是西门（又给他起名叫彼得），和他弟弟安得烈，以及雅各、约翰、腓力、巴多罗迈、
v.ESV: Simon, whom he named Peter, and Andrew his brother, and James and John, and Philip, and Bartholomew,
v.GAE: 곧 베드로라고도 이름을 주신 시몬과 그의 동생 안드레와 야고보와 요한과 빌립과 바돌로매와
v.KJV: Simon, (whom he also named Peter,) and Andrew his brother, James and John, Philip and Bartholomew,
v.KRV: 곧 베드로라고도 이름 주신 시몬과 및 그 형제 안드레와 및 야고보와 요한과 빌립과 바돌로매와
v.NASB: Simon, whom He also named Peter, and Andrew his brother; and James and John; and Philip and Bartholomew;
v.NIV: Simon (whom he named Peter), his brother Andrew, James, John, Philip, Bartholomew,
v.NRSV: Simon, whom he named Peter, and his brother Andrew, and James, and John, and Philip, and Bartholomew,
v.SAENEW: 열둘은 베드로라고도 이름을 주신 시몬과 그의 동생 안드레, 그리고 야고보와 요한과 빌립과 바돌로매와

v.KJV: The disciple is not above his master: but every one that is perfect shall be as his master.

v.NASB: 'A pupil is not above his teacher; but everyone, after he has been fully trained, will be like his teacher.
v.NIV: The student is not above the teacher, but everyone who is fully trained will be like their teacher.
v.NRSV: A disciple is not above the teacher, but everyone who is fully qualified will be like the teacher.
v.SAENEW: 제자는 스승보다 높지 않다. 그러나 누구든지 다 배우고 나면, 자기의 스승과 같이 될 것이다.
v.WLB: 학생이 스승보다 나을 수 없다. 그러나 누구든지 다 배우고 나면 자기 스승과 같이 될 것이다.

v.CNV: 为什么看得见你弟兄眼中的木屑，却想不到自己眼中的梁木呢？
v.ESV: Why do you see the speck that is in your brother's eye, but do not notice the log that is in your own eye?
v.GAE: 어찌하여 형제의 눈 속에 있는 티는 보고 네 눈 속에 있는 들보는 깨닫지 못하느냐
v.KJV: And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother’s eye, but perceivest not the beam that is in thine own eye?
v.KRV: 어찌하여 형제의 눈 속에 있는 티는 보고 네 눈 속에 있는 들보는 깨닫지 못하느냐
v.NASB: 'Why do you look at the speck that is in your brother's eye, but do not notice the log that is in your own eye?
v.NIV: “Why do you look at the speck of sawdust in your brother’s eye and pay no attention to the plank in your own eye?
v.NRSV: Why do you see the speck in your neighbor’s eye, but do not notice the log in your own eye?
v.SAENEW: 어찌하여 너는 남의 눈 속에 있는 티는 보면서, 네 눈 속에 있는 들보는 깨닫지 못하느냐?
v.WLB: 어째서 너는 네 형제의 눈에 있는 티는 보면서 네 눈에 있는 들보는 깨닫지 못하느냐?

v.CNV: 你不看见自己眼中的梁木，怎能对你弟兄说：‘弟兄，容我除去你眼中的木屑’呢？伪君子啊！先去掉自己眼中的梁木，才能看得清楚，好去掉弟兄眼中的木屑。
v.ESV: How can you say to your brother, ‘Brother, let me take out the speck that is in your eye,’ when you yourself do not see the log that is in your own eye? You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take out the speck that is in your brother's eye.
v.GAE: 너는 네 눈 속에 있는 들보를 보지 못하면서 어찌하여 형제에게 말하기를 형제여 나로 네 눈 속에 있는 티를 빼게 하라 할 수 있느냐 외식하는 자여 먼저 네 눈 속에서 들보를 빼라 그 후에야 네가 밝히 보고 형제의 눈 속에 있는 티를 빼리라
v.KJV: Either how canst thou say to thy brother, Brother, let me pull out the mote that is in thine eye, when thou thyself beholdest not the beam that is in thine own eye? Thou hypocrite, cast out first the beam out of thine own eye, and then shalt thou see clearly to pull out the mote that is in thy brother’s eye.
v.KRV: 너는 네 눈 속에 있는 들보를 보지 못하면서 어찌하여 형제에게 말하기를 형제여 나로 네 눈 속에 있는 티를 빼게 하라 할 수 있느냐 외식하는 자여 먼저 네 눈 속에서 들보를 빼어라 그 후에야 네가 밝히 보고 형제의 눈 속에 있는 티를 빼리라
v.NASB: 'Or how can you say to your brother, 'Brother, let me take out the speck that is in your eye,' when you yourself do not see the log that is in your own eye? You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take out the speck that is in your brother's eye.
v.NIV: How can you say to your brother, ‘Brother, let me take the speck out of your eye,’ when you yourself fail to see the plank in your own eye? You hypocrite, first take the plank out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother’s eye.
v.NRSV: Or how can you say to your neighbor, ‘Friend, let me take out the speck in your eye,’ when you yourself do not see the log in your own eye? You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your neighbor’s eye.
v.SAENEW: 너는 네 눈 속에 있는 들보를 보지 못하면서, 어떻게 남에게 '친구야, 내가 네 눈 속에 있는 티를 빼내 줄 테니 가만히 있어라' 하고 말할 수 있겠느냐? 위선자야, 먼저 네 눈에서 들보를 빼내어라. 그리해야 그 때에 네가 똑똑히 보게 되어서, 남의 눈 속에 있는 티를 빼 줄 수 있을 것이다.
v.WLB: 네 눈에 있는 들보는 보지 못하면서 어떻게 형제에게 ‘형제여, 네 눈에 있는 티를 빼자’고 하겠느냐? 위선자여, 먼저 네 눈에서 들보를 빼내라. 그런 후에야 네가 정확히 보고 형제의 눈 속에 있는 티를 빼낼 수 있을 것이다.

v.CNV: “因为好树不能结坏果子，坏树不能结好果子。
v.ESV: “For no good tree bears bad fruit, nor again does a bad tree bear good fruit,
v.GAE: 못된 열매 맺는 좋은 나무가 없고 또 좋은 열매 맺는 못된 나무가 없느니라
v.KJV: For a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit; neither doth a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
v.KRV: 못된 열매 맺는 좋은 나무가 없고 또 좋은 열매 맺는 못된 나무가 없느니라
v.NASB: 'For there is no good tree which produces bad fruit, nor, on the other hand, a bad tree which produces good fruit.
v.NIV: “No good tree bears bad fruit, nor does a bad tree bear good fruit.
v.NRSV: “No good tree bears bad fruit, nor again does a bad tree bear good fruit;
v.SAENEW: 좋은 나무가 나쁜 열매를 맺지 않고, 또 나쁜 나무가 좋은 열매를 맺지 않는다.
v.WLB: 좋은 나무는 나쁜 열매를 맺지 않고 나쁜 나무는 좋은 열매를 맺을 수 없다.

v.CNV: 凭着果子就可以认出树来。人不能从荆棘上采无花果，也不能从蒺藜里摘葡萄。
v.ESV: for each tree is known by its own fruit. For figs are not gathered from thornbushes, nor are grapes picked from a bramble bush.
v.GAE: 나무는 각각 그 열매로 아나니 가시나무에서 무화과를, 또는 찔레에서 포도를 따지 못하느니라
v.KJV: For every tree is known by his own fruit. For of thorns men do not gather figs, nor of a bramble bush gather they grapes.
v.KRV: 나무는 각각 그 열매로 아나니 가시나무에서 무화과를, 또는 찔레에서 포도를 따지 못하느니라
v.NASB: 'For each tree is known by its own fruit. For men do not gather figs from thorns, nor do they pick grapes from a briar bush.
v.NIV: Each tree is recognized by its own fruit. People do not pick figs from thornbushes, or grapes from briers.
v.NRSV: for each tree is known by its own fruit. Figs are not gathered from thorns, nor are grapes picked from a bramble bush.
v.SAENEW: 나무는 각각 그 열매를 보면 안다. 가시나무에서 무화과를 거두어들이지 못하고, 가시덤불에서 포도를 따지 못한다.
v.WLB: 나무마다 그 열매를 보면 안다. 가시나무에서 무화과를 딸 수 없고 찔레나무에서 포도를 딸 수 없는 법이다.

v.CNV: 良善的人从心中所存的良善发出良善，邪恶的人从心中所存的邪恶发出邪恶；因为心中所充满的，口里就说出来。
v.ESV: The good person out of the good treasure of his heart produces good, and the evil person out of his evil treasure produces evil, for out of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaks.
v.GAE: 선한 사람은 마음에 쌓은 선에서 선을 내고 악한 자는 그 쌓은 악에서 악을 내나니 이는 마음에 가득한 것을 입으로 말함이니라
v.KJV: A good man out of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is good; and an evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is evil: for of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaketh.
v.KRV: 선한 사람은 마음의 쌓은 선에서 선을 내고 악한 자는 그 쌓은 악에서 악을 내나니 이는 마음의 가득한 것을 입으로 말함이니라
v.NASB: 'The good man out of the good treasure of his heart brings forth what is good; and the evil man out of the evil treasure brings forth what is evil; for his mouth speaks from that which fills his heart.
v.NIV: A good man brings good things out of the good stored up in his heart, and an evil man brings evil things out of the evil stored up in his heart. For the mouth speaks what the heart is full of.
v.NRSV: The good person out of the good treasure of the heart produces good, and the evil person out of evil treasure produces evil; for it is out of the abundance of the heart that the mouth speaks.
v.SAENEW: 선한 사람은 그 마음 속에 갈무리해 놓은 선 더미에서 선한 것을 내고, 악한 사람은 그 마음 속에 갈무리해 놓은 악 더미에서 악한 것을 낸다. 마음에 가득 찬 것을 입으로 말하는 법이다.
v.WLB: 선한 사람은 마음속에 선한 것을 두었다가 선한 것을 내놓고 악한 사람은 마음속에 악한 것을 두었다가 악한 것을 내놓는다. 사람은 마음에 가득 찬 것을 입으로 말하는 법이다.

v.CNV: “你们为什么称呼我‘主啊！主啊！’却不遵行我的吩咐呢？
v.ESV: “Why do you call me ‘Lord, Lord,’ and not do what I tell you?
v.GAE: 너희는 나를 불러 주여 주여 하면서도 어찌하여 내가 말하는 것을 행하지 아니하느냐
v.KJV: And why call ye me, Lord, Lord, and do not the things which I say?
v.KRV: 너희는 나를 불러 주여 주여 하면서도 어찌하여 나의 말하는 것을 행치 아니하느냐
v.NASB: 'Why do you call Me, 'Lord, Lord,' and do not do what I say?
v.NIV: “Why do you call me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ and do not do what I say?
v.NRSV: “Why do you call me ‘Lord, Lord,’ and do not do what I tell you?
v.SAENEW: 어찌하여 너희는 나더러 '주님, 주님!' 하면서도, 내가 말하는 것은 행하지 않느냐?
v.WLB: 어째서 너희는 나를 ‘주여, 주여’ 하고 부르면서 내가 말하는 것은 행하지 않느냐?

v.CNV: 每一个到我跟前，听我的话并且去行的，我要指示你们他像什么人。
v.ESV: Everyone who comes to me and hears my words and does them, I will show you what he is like:
v.GAE: 내게 나아와 내 말을 듣고 행하는 자마다 누구와 같은 것을 너희에게 보이리라
v.KJV: Whosoever cometh to me, and heareth my sayings, and doeth them, I will shew you to whom he is like:
v.KRV: 내게 나아와 내 말을 듣고 행하는 자마다 누구와 같은 것을 너희에게 보이리라
v.NASB: 'Everyone who comes to Me and hears My words and acts on them, I will show you whom he is like:
v.NIV: As for everyone who comes to me and hears my words and puts them into practice, I will show you what they are like.
v.NRSV: I will show you what someone is like who comes to me, hears my words, and acts on them.
v.SAENEW: 내게 와서 내 말을 듣고 그대로 행하는 사람이 어떤 사람과 같은지를 너희에게 보여 주겠다.
v.WLB: 내게 와서 내 말을 듣고 그대로 실천에 옮기는 사람이 어떤 사람과 같은지 너희에게 보여 주겠다.

v.CNV: 他像一个人建造房屋，挖深了地，把根基建在磐石上。大水泛滥的时候，急流冲击那房屋，不能使它动摇，因为它建造得好。
v.ESV: he is like a man building a house, who dug deep and laid the foundation on the rock. And when a flood arose, the stream broke against that house and could not shake it, because it had been well built.
v.GAE: 집을 짓되 깊이 파고 주추를 반석 위에 놓은 사람과 같으니 큰 물이 나서 탁류가 그 집에 부딪치되 잘 지었기 때문에 능히 요동하지 못하게 하였거니와
v.KJV: He is like a man which built an house, and digged deep, and laid the foundation on a rock: and when the flood arose, the stream beat vehemently upon that house, and could not shake it: for it was founded upon a rock.
v.KRV: 집을 짓되 깊이 파고 주초를 반석 위에 놓은 사람과 같으니 큰 물이 나서 탁류가 그 집에 부딪히되 잘 지은 연고로 능히 요동케 못하였거니와
v.NASB: he is like a man building a house, who dug deep and laid a foundation on the rock; and when a flood occurred, the torrent burst against that house and could not shake it, because it had been well built.
v.NIV: They are like a man building a house, who dug down deep and laid the foundation on rock. When a flood came, the torrent struck that house but could not shake it, because it was well built.
v.NRSV: That one is like a man building a house, who dug deeply and laid the foundation on rock; when a flood arose, the river burst against that house but could not shake it, because it had been well built.
v.SAENEW: 그는 땅을 깊이 파고, 반석 위에다 기초를 놓고 집을 짓는 사람과 같다. 홍수가 나서 물살이 그 집에 들이쳐도, 그 집은 흔들리지도 않는다. 잘 지은 집이기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 그는 땅을 깊이 파고 바위 위에 단단히 기초를 세운 건축자와 같다. 홍수가 나서 폭우가 덮쳐도 그 집은 흔들리지 않았다. 그 집이 잘 지어졌기 때문이다.

v.CNV: 但那听见而不遵行的，就像人在地上建屋，没有根基，急流一冲，就立刻倒塌，毁坏得很厉害。”
v.ESV: But the one who hears and does not do them is like a man who built a house on the ground without a foundation. When the stream broke against it, immediately it fell, and the ruin of that house was great.”
v.GAE: 듣고 행하지 아니하는 자는 주추 없이 흙 위에 집 지은 사람과 같으니 탁류가 부딪치매 집이 곧 무너져 파괴됨이 심하니라 하시니라
v.KJV: But he that heareth, and doeth not, is like a man that without a foundation built an house upon the earth; against which the stream did beat vehemently, and immediately it fell; and the ruin of that house was great.
v.KRV: 듣고 행치 아니하는 자는 주초 없이 흙 위에 집 지은 사람과 같으니 탁류가 부딪히매 집이 곧 무너져 파괴됨이 심하니라 하시니라
v.NASB: 'But the one who has heard and has not acted accordingly, is like a man who built a house on the ground without any foundation; and the torrent burst against it and immediately it collapsed, and the ruin of that house was great.'
v.NIV: But the one who hears my words and does not put them into practice is like a man who built a house on the ground without a foundation. The moment the torrent struck that house, it collapsed and its destruction was complete.”
v.NRSV: But the one who hears and does not act is like a man who built a house on the ground without a foundation. When the river burst against it, immediately it fell, and great was the ruin of that house.”
v.SAENEW: 그러나 내 말을 듣고서도 그대로 행하지 않는 사람은, 기초 없이 맨 흙 위에다가 집을 지은 사람과 같다. 물살이 그 집에 들이치니, 그 집은 곧 무너져 버렸고, 그 집의 무너짐이 엄청났다."
v.WLB: 그러나 내 말을 듣고도 실천에 옮기지 않는 사람은 기초 없이 맨땅에 집을 지은 사람과 같다. 그 집은 폭우가 덮치는 즉시 무너져 폭삭 주저앉았다.”
